WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-01: Search Engine Discovery & Reconnaissance
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Use search engines to find exposed information, internal paths, credentials, or sensitive documents indexed publicly.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-02: Fingerprint Web Server
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Identify the web server type, version, and underlying OS via banners, headers, and error responses.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-03: Review Webserver Metafiles for Information Leakage
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Examine robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and other metafiles for sensitive path disclosures.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-04: Enumerate Applications on Webserver
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Identify all web applications hosted on the same server via port scanning and virtual host enumeration.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-05: Review Webpage Content for Information Leakage
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Inspect HTML comments, hidden fields, JavaScript files, and source code for sensitive information.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-06: Identify Application Entry Points
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Map all user-controlled input vectors: forms, query parameters, headers, cookies, API endpoints.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-07: Map Execution Paths Through Application
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Trace application workflows and identify all code paths that could process attacker-controlled input.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-08: Fingerprint Web Application Framework
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Identify the framework and version (Django, Rails, Laravel, etc.) via headers, cookies, and error pages.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-09: Fingerprint Web Application
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Identify the CMS or application type (WordPress, Drupal, etc.) and enumerate installed plugins/themes.

WSTG - Information Gathering.label: WSTG-INFO-10: Map Application Architecture
WSTG - Information Gathering.description: Identify load balancers, WAFs, CDNs, reverse proxies, and backend services in the application stack.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-01: Test Network Infrastructure Configuration
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Review network components for misconfigurations: open ports, unpatched services, weak cipher suites.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-02: Test Application Platform Configuration
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Check web server and application server config for default settings, unnecessary modules, debug modes.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-03: Test File Extension Handling
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Verify how the server handles unexpected file extensions — check for source code disclosure or execution.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-04: Review Backup & Unreferenced Files
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Discover backup files (.bak, .old, ~), unreferenced pages, and forgotten test files that leak source or config.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-05: Enumerate Admin Interfaces
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Find admin panels, management consoles, and privileged endpoints exposed on the server.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-06: Test HTTP Methods
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Enumerate enabled HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE) and test for dangerous method abuse.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-07: Test HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS)
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Verify HSTS header is present, has appropriate max-age, and includes subdomains where applicable.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-08: Test RIA Cross Domain Policy
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Review crossdomain.xml and clientaccesspolicy.xml for overly permissive cross-domain access.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-09: Test File Permissions
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Verify server files and directories have appropriate permissions; check for world-writable paths.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-10: Test for Subdomain Takeover
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Identify dangling DNS entries pointing to deprovisioned cloud/SaaS resources susceptible to takeover.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-11: Test Cloud Storage
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Check S3 buckets, Azure blobs, and GCS buckets for public read/write access and sensitive data exposure.

WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.label: WSTG-CONF-12: Test Content Security Policy (CSP)
WSTG - Configuration & Deployment Manage.description: Evaluate the CSP header for overly permissive directives, unsafe-inline, unsafe-eval, or missing directives.

WSTG - Identity Management.label: WSTG-IDNT-01: Test Role Definitions
WSTG - Identity Management.description: Verify roles are clearly defined and enforced; check for role confusion or privilege overlap.

WSTG - Identity Management.label: WSTG-IDNT-02: Test User Registration Process
WSTG - Identity Management.description: Test registration for account enumeration, weak validation, and ability to register with privileged identifiers.

WSTG - Identity Management.label: WSTG-IDNT-03: Test Account Provisioning Process
WSTG - Identity Management.description: Verify provisioning workflows require proper authorization and cannot be abused to escalate privileges.

WSTG - Identity Management.label: WSTG-IDNT-04: Test for Account Enumeration
WSTG - Identity Management.description: Determine if the application leaks valid usernames via login errors, registration, or password reset responses.

WSTG - Identity Management.label: WSTG-IDNT-05: Test for Weak Username Policy
WSTG - Identity Management.description: Check if usernames are guessable (sequential IDs, email addresses) and if the policy enforces uniqueness.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-01: Test Credentials Over Encrypted Channel
WSTG - Authentication.description: Confirm all authentication occurs over HTTPS; check for mixed content, HTTP fallback, or credential leakage.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-02: Test Default Credentials
WSTG - Authentication.description: Attempt login with vendor-supplied default credentials on all services and admin interfaces.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-03: Test Account Lockout Mechanism
WSTG - Authentication.description: Test brute-force protections: lockout thresholds, reset behaviour, IP-based versus account-based lockout.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-04: Test for Authentication Bypass
WSTG - Authentication.description: Attempt to bypass authentication via parameter manipulation, forced browsing, or request forgery.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-05: Test Remember Password Functionality
WSTG - Authentication.description: Inspect remember-me tokens for weak entropy, insecure storage, or indefinite validity.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-06: Test Browser Cache Weaknesses
WSTG - Authentication.description: Verify sensitive pages set Cache-Control: no-store and are not retrievable from browser back button.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-07: Test Weak Password Policy
WSTG - Authentication.description: Review minimum length, complexity requirements, password reuse policy, and common password rejection.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-08: Test Security Questions
WSTG - Authentication.description: Evaluate secret question/answer mechanisms for guessability, enumeration, and bypass potential.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-09: Test Password Reset Functionality
WSTG - Authentication.description: Test reset flows for token predictability, reuse, expiry, account enumeration, and host header injection.

WSTG - Authentication.label: WSTG-ATHN-10: Test Alternative Authentication Channels
WSTG - Authentication.description: Identify weaker authentication paths (mobile app, API, legacy portal) that bypass primary controls.

WSTG - Authorization.label: WSTG-ATHZ-01: Test Directory Traversal / File Include
WSTG - Authorization.description: Test for path traversal in file parameters; attempt to read arbitrary files or include remote content.

WSTG - Authorization.label: WSTG-ATHZ-02: Test for Authorization Schema Bypass
WSTG - Authorization.description: Attempt to access privileged functions or resources by manipulating roles, parameters, or HTTP methods.

WSTG - Authorization.label: WSTG-ATHZ-03: Test for Privilege Escalation
WSTG - Authorization.description: Attempt horizontal and vertical privilege escalation by modifying user identifiers or role parameters.

WSTG - Authorization.label: WSTG-ATHZ-04: Test for Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR)
WSTG - Authorization.description: Access objects (records, files, accounts) belonging to other users by manipulating object identifiers.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-01: Test Session Management Schema
WSTG - Session Management.description: Analyse session token entropy, predictability, and uniqueness; verify tokens are regenerated after login.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-02: Test Cookie Attributes
WSTG - Session Management.description: Verify session cookies have Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite attributes and appropriate scope/expiry.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-03: Test for Session Fixation
WSTG - Session Management.description: Determine if the application accepts attacker-supplied session tokens or fails to rotate them on login.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-04: Test for Exposed Session Variables
WSTG - Session Management.description: Check if session tokens appear in URLs, Referer headers, logs, or are otherwise improperly exposed.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-05: Test for CSRF
WSTG - Session Management.description: Verify CSRF tokens are present, unique per session, and validated for all state-changing requests.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-06: Test Logout Functionality
WSTG - Session Management.description: Confirm logout invalidates the server-side session and that tokens cannot be reused post-logout.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-07: Test Session Timeout
WSTG - Session Management.description: Verify idle and absolute session timeouts are enforced server-side and sessions expire appropriately.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-08: Test for Session Puzzling
WSTG - Session Management.description: Check if session variables are used in multiple contexts allowing session variable overloading attacks.

WSTG - Session Management.label: WSTG-SESS-09: Test for Session Hijacking
WSTG - Session Management.description: Assess susceptibility to session hijacking via network sniffing, XSS-based token theft, or token prediction.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-01: Test for Reflected XSS
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject script payloads into all reflected input vectors; verify output encoding and CSP effectiveness.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-02: Test for Stored XSS
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Submit persistent script payloads via all stored input fields; verify they are not rendered unescaped.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-03: Test for HTTP Verb Tampering
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test for authentication/authorisation bypass by switching HTTP methods (GET vs POST vs HEAD).

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-04: Test for HTTP Parameter Pollution
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Submit duplicate parameters to confuse server-side parsing and bypass input validation or WAF rules.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-05: Test for SQL Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test all database-backed inputs for error-based, blind, time-based, and out-of-band SQL injection.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-06: Test for LDAP Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject LDAP metacharacters into authentication and search inputs to bypass controls or enumerate directory.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-07: Test for XML Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test XML inputs for injection, XXE (external entity), and XPath injection vulnerabilities.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-08: Test for SSI Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Check for Server-Side Include injection in inputs that are embedded in server-parsed HTML files.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-09: Test for XPath Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject XPath syntax into inputs used to query XML data stores; attempt authentication bypass or data extraction.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-10: Test for IMAP/SMTP Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject mail headers and commands via webmail interfaces to relay spam, access mailboxes, or enumerate users.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-11: Test for Code Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test inputs used in dynamic code execution (eval, exec) for arbitrary code injection.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-12: Test for Command Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject OS commands via inputs passed to shell functions; test for blind injection via time delays or OOB.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-13: Test for Format String Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test inputs used in printf-style format functions for format string vulnerabilities.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-14: Test for Incubated Vulnerability
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test for vulnerabilities that are triggered asynchronously or by a different user/process than the injector.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-15: Test for HTTP Splitting/Smuggling
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject CRLF sequences into HTTP headers; test for request smuggling between front-end and back-end servers.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-16: Test for HTTP Incoming Requests
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Monitor and analyse all incoming HTTP requests for unexpected parameters, headers, or malformed input.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-17: Test for Host Header Injection
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject arbitrary Host headers to trigger password reset poisoning, cache poisoning, or SSRF.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-18: Test for Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI)
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Inject template syntax into inputs rendered by template engines (Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker) to achieve RCE.

WSTG - Input Validation.label: WSTG-INPV-19: Test for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
WSTG - Input Validation.description: Test inputs that trigger server-side HTTP requests for SSRF to internal services or cloud metadata endpoints.

WSTG - Error Handling.label: WSTG-ERRH-01: Test for Improper Error Handling
WSTG - Error Handling.description: Trigger application errors and verify responses don't leak stack traces, internal paths, or DB structure.

WSTG - Error Handling.label: WSTG-ERRH-02: Test for Stack Traces
WSTG - Error Handling.description: Confirm production error pages show generic messages and that verbose errors are only in development mode.

WSTG - Cryptography.label: WSTG-CRYP-01: Test Weak Transport Layer Security
WSTG - Cryptography.description: Scan for weak TLS versions (SSLv3, TLS 1.0/1.1), weak cipher suites, expired certs, and missing features.

WSTG - Cryptography.label: WSTG-CRYP-02: Test for Padding Oracle
WSTG - Cryptography.description: Test CBC-mode encrypted tokens for padding oracle vulnerabilities enabling decryption or forgery.

WSTG - Cryptography.label: WSTG-CRYP-03: Test for Sensitive Data Over Unencrypted Channel
WSTG - Cryptography.description: Verify no sensitive data (credentials, PII, tokens) is transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.

WSTG - Cryptography.label: WSTG-CRYP-04: Test for Weak Encryption
WSTG - Cryptography.description: Identify use of weak or broken algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, ECB mode) for sensitive data storage or transit.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-01: Test Business Logic Data Validation
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Verify the application enforces business rules on all input, not just UI-level controls.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-02: Test Ability to Forge Requests
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Replay or modify legitimate requests to perform unintended actions (e.g., negative prices, skipped steps).

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-03: Test Integrity Checks
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Verify the application detects and rejects tampered data; check signatures, checksums, and MAC validation.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-04: Test for Process Timing
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Test for race conditions and time-of-check/time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerabilities in critical operations.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-05: Test Function Use Limits
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Verify rate limits and usage caps are enforced server-side for sensitive functions (OTP, voucher codes).

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-06: Test for Workflow Circumvention
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Attempt to skip required workflow steps (payment, verification) by directly accessing later-stage URLs.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-07: Test Defences Against Application Misuse
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Attempt to abuse legitimate features in unintended ways; verify anomaly detection or rate limiting exists.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-08: Test Upload of Unexpected File Types
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Upload non-whitelisted file types to upload functions; check for client-side-only MIME type validation.

WSTG - Business Logic.label: WSTG-BUSL-09: Test Upload of Malicious Files
WSTG - Business Logic.description: Upload web shells, polyglots, and malicious files; check if uploaded files are executed by the server.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-01: Test for DOM-Based XSS
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Identify sinks that write attacker-controlled data to the DOM (innerHTML, document.write, eval) without sanitisation.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-02: Test for JavaScript Execution
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Test inputs that flow into JavaScript execution contexts for injection via prototype pollution or eval sinks.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-03: Test for HTML Injection
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Inject HTML markup into inputs reflected in responses; test for phishing and UI redressing potential.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-04: Test for Client-Side URL Redirect
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Test open redirect vulnerabilities in client-side redirect parameters for phishing and token theft.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-05: Test for CSS Injection
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Inject CSS into inputs rendered in style contexts; check for data exfiltration via CSS selectors.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-06: Test for Client-Side Resource Manipulation
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Test inputs controlling resource URLs (scripts, stylesheets) for manipulation to load attacker content.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-07: Test Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Verify CORS policy does not allow arbitrary origins to read sensitive responses; check ACAO: * on credentialed requests.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-08: Test for Clickjacking
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Verify X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors prevents the application from being embedded in iframes.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-09: Test WebSockets
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Test WebSocket connections for lack of authentication, missing origin validation, and injection vulnerabilities.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-10: Test Web Messaging
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Review postMessage handlers for missing origin validation allowing cross-origin message injection.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-11: Test Browser Storage
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Review localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB for sensitive data stored client-side without encryption.

WSTG - Client-Side.label: WSTG-CLNT-12: Test for Cross-Site Script Inclusion (XSSI)
WSTG - Client-Side.description: Test JSON endpoints for data leakage when included as scripts in attacker-controlled pages.

WSTG - API Testing.label: WSTG-APIT-01: Test GraphQL
WSTG - API Testing.description: Test GraphQL endpoints for introspection exposure, query depth attacks, batching abuse, and injection.

WSTG - API Testing.label: WSTG-APIT-02: Test REST
WSTG - API Testing.description: Test REST APIs for broken object-level auth, mass assignment, improper HTTP method handling, and injection.

WSTG - API Testing.label: WSTG-APIT-03: Test SOAP/XML
WSTG - API Testing.description: Test SOAP services for XXE, WSDL enumeration, parameter tampering, and authentication weaknesses.
